failure_modes.text: Generic offers blur segment differences and leave the business sounding like every competitor.
failure_modes.mitigation: Segment messaging around genuine value differences: customize offers based on what each segment actually needs, not just willingness to pay.

failure_modes.text: Tiering can backfire if customers can compare treatment easily and judge it as unfair.
failure_modes.mitigation: Anticipate transparency: design tiering so that if all customers saw all tiers simultaneously, the differentiation would feel fair rather than exploitative.

failure_modes.text: Short-term extraction can damage long-term trust and willingness to buy later.
failure_modes.mitigation: Balance extraction against relationship: cap short-term pricing tactics where they would erode long-term trust, repeat purchase, or referral willingness.

premortem_questions.text: Compared to what does this offer look like a clear bargain or a risky mistake?
What would each segment have to believe to accept this price and structure?
premortem_questions.source_quote: What would the customer have to believe

premortem_questions.text: Where could segmentation create confusion, arbitrage, or trust damage?

heuristics.text: Define one specific problem for one specific type of buyer in one specific way.
heuristics.source_quote: 1 Specific Problem, for 1 Specific Type Of Person, in 1 Specific Way

heuristics.text: Translate every feature into a segment-specific benefit before discussing price.
Tailor the framing and voice of the offer to the buyer's processing style.
